title: Cariprazine (Vraylar) in Pediatric Bipolar I Depression

sections.title: THE HOOK
sections.body:
STILL watching a 13-year-old sink into bipolar depression while the FDA only
approves three drugs for this age group?

sections.title: THE UNMET NEED
sections.body:
Pediatric bipolar depression affects ~1% of youth and drives the highest suicide risk in child psychiatry. Only
lurasidone, olanzapine-fluoxetine, and lumateperone carry pediatric bipolar-depression labels. Parents and
clinicians are left choosing between metabolic risk and weight gain.
H A D2/D3 partial agonist with a clean metabolic profile could reset the pediatric
bipolar-depression standard of care.

sections.title: AT A GLANCE
sections.body:
10-17 6 wks 1.5-3 mg ~380
AGE RANGE DOUBLE-BLIND FLEX DOSE TARGET N

sections.title: WHY THIS STUDY EXISTS
sections.body:
Cariprazine is already approved for adult bipolar I depression, mania, schizophrenia, and MDD adjunct. This
Phase 3 bridges to pediatric label — a gap the field has begged to fill.

sections.title: MECHANISM OF ACTION
sections.body:
D3-preferring D2/D3 partial agonist + 5-HT1A partial agonist + 5-HT2B antagonist. Long half-life (1-3 weeks
for active metabolite) provides forgiving dosing in adolescents prone to missing doses.

sections.title: STUDY DESIGN DEEP DIVE
sections.body:
Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, flexible-dose 6-week monotherapy study. Primary endpoint:
change in CDRS-R total. Key secondary: CGI-BP-S depression. Includes 26-week open-label extension.

sections.title: RECRUIT IF YOUR PATIENT…
sections.body:
Your patient is 10-17, has a confirmed DSM-5 Bipolar I diagnosis, is currently in a major depressive episode
(CDRS-R >=45), and hasn't responded to or tolerated first-line options. The whole point: catch bipolar
depression before suicide attempts and school failure compound.

sections.title: STOP / EXCLUDE IF…
sections.body:
7 Active suicidal plan/intent (C-SSRS item 4-5 past month)
7 Substance use disorder past 6 months (except nicotine)


7 History of NMS or tardive dyskinesia
7 Pregnancy/nursing; sexually active without contraception
7 Prior cariprazine failure or intolerance

sections.title: IDEAL PATIENT PROFILE
sections.body:
13-16 y/o with early-onset BP-I, currently depressed, on a mood stabilizer that isn't working, family frustrated
with weight gain from atypicals.

sections.title: HOW IPMG FINDS THESE PATIENTS (ECW + ICD-10)
sections.body:
eCW query: ICD-10 F31.3x / F31.4 / F31.5 + age 10-17 + any antidepressant/antipsychotic in last 90 days.
Flag 'weight gain' chief complaints.

sections.title: COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE
sections.body:
Lurasidone (Latuda) pediatric BP-D approved 2018; olanzapine-fluoxetine (Symbyax) 10+; lumateperone
pediatric pending. Cariprazine differentiates on metabolic neutrality + once-daily long half-life.

sections.title: RISK / BENEFIT LOGIC
sections.body:
Benefit: first D3-preferring option for youth BP-D, minimal weight/metabolic burden. Risk: akathisia
(10-15%), somnolence, EPS. Manageable with slow titration.

sections.title: WHY THIS MATTERS FOR A LARGE PSYCHIATRIC GROUP
sections.body:
IPMG's pediatric volume across 4 sites means we can realistically enroll 15-20 patients — meaningful
contribution to a ~380-patient trial and a gateway to pediatric research credentials.

sections.title: PROVIDER TALK TRACK (6TH–7TH GRADE)
sections.body:
“We have a study for kids 10 to 17 with bipolar depression. The medicine is already approved for adults.
We're checking if it works for teens without the weight gain that other medicines cause.”

sections.body:

n FLAG. Mark eligible charts with a sticky note or task for the PMHNP team. Tag with the comment code.

sections.title: THE HOOK
sections.body:
STILL watching TRD patients cycle through 3 SSRIs, an SNRI, and an atypical
before anyone offers a novel mechanism?

sections.title: THE UNMET NEED
sections.body:
Two-thirds of MDD patients fail to remit on first-line SSRIs, and for those with true treatment-resistant
depression (TRD), every additional failed trial compounds demoralization and disability. The field needs
novel non-monoaminergic mechanisms for patients already on an approved antidepressant.
H A once-daily oral adjunct with a non-monoaminergic mechanism could replace atypical
augmentation as the go-to TRD next step.

sections.title: AT A GLANCE
sections.body:
18-65 6 wks Once daily ~500
AGE DB PHASE DOSING TARGET N

sections.title: WHY THIS STUDY EXISTS
sections.body:
Acadia built its psychiatry franchise on pimavanserin (Nuplazid). ACP-211 is their swing at
treatment-resistant depression with a differentiated MOA.

sections.title: MECHANISM OF ACTION
sections.body:
Novel serotonergic/glutamatergic modulator (Acadia-proprietary). Designed to engage mood and anhedonia
circuits in patients who have already failed standard antidepressants.

sections.title: STUDY DESIGN DEEP DIVE
sections.body:
Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled adjunctive Phase 2/3 in TRD. Patients remain on their current
FDA-approved antidepressant and add ACP-211 or placebo. Primary: MADRS change at Week 6. Key
secondary: SHAPS (anhedonia), CGI-S, response/remission.

sections.title: RECRUIT IF YOUR PATIENT…
sections.body:
Adults 18-65 with moderate-to-severe MDD (MADRS >=26), current episode >=8 weeks, CURRENTLY
TREATED with an FDA-approved antidepressant at adequate dose/duration, AND documented history of
inadequate response to at least 2 prior antidepressant trials in the current episode. The whole point: TRD is


the target — screen IN, not out.

sections.title: STOP / EXCLUDE IF…
sections.body:
7 Bipolar, psychotic, or substance use disorder
7 Active suicidality (C-SSRS 4-5)
7 Pregnancy/nursing
7 Recent psychedelic or ketamine exposure
7 Currently antidepressant-naive or off antidepressants at screening

sections.title: IDEAL PATIENT PROFILE
sections.body:
48-year-old TRD patient on venlafaxine 225 mg x 10 weeks, already tried sertraline and escitalopram at full
dose this episode, PHQ-9 stuck at 18.

sections.title: HOW IPMG FINDS THESE PATIENTS (ECW + ICD-10)
sections.body:
eCW: ICD-10 F32.1/F32.2/F33.1/F33.2, PHQ-9 >=15 in last 90 days, CURRENTLY on an antidepressant,
with medication history showing >=2 prior antidepressant trials this episode. Flag 'refractory' or 'not
responding' in HPI.

sections.title: COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE
sections.body:
Auvelity (dextromethorphan-bupropion), Spravato, Exxua (gepirone), and aripiprazole/brexpiprazole
augmentation all compete for the TRD adjunct space. ACP-211 needs clean adjunctive data to separate
from the pack.

sections.title: RISK / BENEFIT LOGIC
sections.body:
Benefit: potential novel non-monoaminergic adjunct, no need to switch the patient off their existing
antidepressant. Risk: unknown long-term safety, Phase 2/3 efficacy unproven. Adjunct design preserves
baseline coverage but adds a new side-effect profile.

sections.title: WHY THIS MATTERS FOR A LARGE PSYCHIATRIC GROUP
sections.body:
TRD is every psychiatrist's daily problem. A site that can consistently enroll TRD adjunct studies is always in
demand.

sections.title: PROVIDER TALK TRACK (6TH–7TH GRADE)
sections.body:
“This is a brand-new kind of add-on depression medicine. You'll stay on your current antidepressant
and add this or a placebo. It doesn't work like Prozac or Zoloft — it works on different brain circuits.”

sections.title: THE HOOK
sections.body: STILL using the same three bipolar-depression drugs from 2013?

sections.title: THE UNMET NEED
sections.body:
Bipolar depression accounts for 70% of BP morbidity. Lurasidone, quetiapine, lumateperone, and
cariprazine are the only labeled options — none address cognitive deficits or adult neurogenesis.
H A pro-neurogenic small molecule could be the first mechanism that actually heals
depressive circuits in BP patients.

sections.title: AT A GLANCE
sections.body:
18-65 6 wks BP-I/II ~200
AGE DB BOTH TARGET N

sections.title: WHY THIS STUDY EXISTS
sections.body:
Alto Neuroscience uses EEG biomarker stratification to pre-select responders. ALTO-100 is their lead
pro-neurogenic asset.

sections.title: MECHANISM OF ACTION
sections.body:
Pro-neurogenic, pro-plasticity small molecule; enhances adult hippocampal neurogenesis and BDNF
signaling. Orally bioavailable, once-daily.

sections.title: STUDY DESIGN DEEP DIVE
sections.body:
Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, biomarker-enriched (EEG-based). 6-week double-blind
treatment period with primary MADRS endpoint at Week 6, followed by an open-label period of up to 7
weeks. Adjunct to stable mood stabilizer or antipsychotic.

sections.title: RECRUIT IF YOUR PATIENT…
sections.body:
Adult BP-I or BP-II, currently depressed (MADRS >=24), on stable mood stabilizer >=4 weeks. The whole
point: help patients whose depression returns despite mood-stabilizer therapy.

sections.title: STOP / EXCLUDE IF…
sections.body:
7 Current mania/hypomania/mixed features


7 Rapid cycling (>=4 episodes/year)
7 Active psychosis
7 Substance use past 6 months
7 Active suicidality C-SSRS 4-5

sections.title: IDEAL PATIENT PROFILE
sections.body: 42 y/o BP-II on lamotrigine, depressed 3 months, reluctant to add quetiapine due to sedation.

sections.title: HOW IPMG FINDS THESE PATIENTS (ECW + ICD-10)
sections.body:
eCW: F31.3x/F31.4/F31.5/F31.81 + mood stabilizer prescription. Screen for current depressive episode in
last PHQ-9.

sections.title: COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE
sections.body:
Caplyta (lumateperone), Latuda (lurasidone), quetiapine, Vraylar hold the adjunct space. ALTO-100's
pro-neurogenic MOA and EEG pre-selection are the differentiators.

sections.title: RISK / BENEFIT LOGIC
sections.body:
Benefit: novel regenerative mechanism, biomarker-matched responders. Risk: unproven at Phase 2/3;
adjunct design means baseline meds still contribute side effects.

sections.title: WHY THIS MATTERS FOR A LARGE PSYCHIATRIC GROUP
sections.body:
BP-D is underserved by pharma pipelines. Owning a BP-D site with EEG infrastructure creates durable
competitive advantage.

sections.title: PROVIDER TALK TRACK (6TH–7TH GRADE)
sections.body:
“This is a new medicine being added to your mood stabilizer. It's designed to help your brain make new
connections — not just mask symptoms.”

title: Solriamfetol in MDD + Excessive Daytime Sleepiness (CLARITY)

sections.title: THE HOOK
sections.body:
STILL treating depression while your patient sleeps 11 hours and still can't stay
awake at work?

sections.title: THE UNMET NEED
sections.body:
40% of MDD patients have excessive daytime sleepiness (EDS) — residual after SSRI response. No drug is
labeled for EDS-in-MDD.
H A DNRI + TAAR1 agonist that treats depression AND wakefulness in one pill could redefine
the residual-symptom endpoint.

sections.title: AT A GLANCE
sections.body:
18-65 Open-label
lead-in 150/300 mg ~450
AGE THEN RW DOSE TARGET N

sections.title: WHY THIS STUDY EXISTS
sections.body:
Axsome bought Sunosi (solriamfetol) from Jazz and is expanding beyond narcolepsy. CLARITY is the
registrational MDD+EDS program.

sections.title: MECHANISM OF ACTION
sections.body:
Dopamine-norepinephrine reuptake inhibitor (DNRI) + TAAR1 agonism. No opioid, no Schedule II
amphetamine profile (Schedule IV).

sections.title: STUDY DESIGN DEEP DIVE
sections.body:
Randomized-withdrawal Phase 3. All eligible participants FIRST receive open-label solriamfetol during a
lead-in period — every patient is guaranteed active drug at the start. Only participants who achieve a
protocol-defined clinical response are then randomized 1:1 to either continue solriamfetol or withdraw to
placebo for the double-blind maintenance phase. Primary: MADRS change / time to relapse. Key secondary:
Epworth Sleepiness Scale, PROMIS-Fatigue.

sections.title: RECRUIT IF YOUR PATIENT…
sections.body:
Adult MDD (MADRS >=26) with EDS (ESS >=10), on stable antidepressant >=6 weeks with partial
response. The whole point: tackle the fatigue that makes 'recovered' depression still feel disabling — and
every enrolled patient gets active drug at the start.

sections.title: STOP / EXCLUDE IF…
sections.body:
7 Narcolepsy/OSA/other primary sleep disorder
7 Substance use past 6 months
7 Uncontrolled hypertension (systolic >140)
7 Pregnancy/nursing
7 MAOI past 14 days

sections.title: IDEAL PATIENT PROFILE
sections.body: 38 y/o on sertraline 150 mg for 8 weeks, PHQ-9 down from 22 to 14, sleeps 10+ hours and still exhausted.

sections.title: HOW IPMG FINDS THESE PATIENTS (ECW + ICD-10)
sections.body: eCW: ICD-10 F32.x/F33.x + G47.10 (hypersomnia) OR complaint of fatigue/EDS in last 3 visits.

sections.title: COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE
sections.body:
Auvelity, Caplyta adjunct, Rexulti adjunct compete for MDD augmentation. Solriamfetol owns the EDS axis
— no direct competitor.

sections.title: RISK / BENEFIT LOGIC
sections.body:
Benefit: dual symptom coverage (mood + wakefulness), existing safety database from Sunosi, every patient
starts on active drug. Risk: BP elevation, insomnia if dosed late, headache. Responders randomized to
placebo face relapse risk in withdrawal phase.

sections.title: WHY THIS MATTERS FOR A LARGE PSYCHIATRIC GROUP
sections.body:
EDS is reported in 1 of every 3 MDD visits. Offering a study matched to that chief complaint — and one
where every patient tries active drug first — converts at a much higher rate than generic MDD recruitment.

sections.title: PROVIDER TALK TRACK (6TH–7TH GRADE)
sections.body:
“This study has two parts. First, everyone gets the real medicine for several weeks. If it helps, you then
have a 50/50 chance of staying on it or switching to a placebo. That means everyone at least tries the
actual drug.”

n FLAG. Mark eligible charts with a sticky note or task for the PMHNP team. Tag with the comment code.
% CALL. IPMG Research at (909) 955-5865 and reference comment CLARITY.

sections.title: THE HOOK
sections.body: STILL waiting for the first Kv7 channel opener to land in psychiatry?

sections.title: THE UNMET NEED
sections.body:
MDD remission rates plateau at 30%. Ion-channel modulation is a virtually untouched MDD target beyond
ketamine.
H If Kv7 activators stabilize hyperexcitable limbic circuits, they could rewrite the depression
playbook.

sections.title: AT A GLANCE
sections.body:
18-75 52 wks Once daily ~400
AGE OPEN-LABEL DOSING TARGET N

sections.title: WHY THIS STUDY EXISTS
sections.body:
Biohaven's Kv7 program followed Nurtec's success. Long-term safety enrollment supports NDA — BUT
note: Phase 2 MDD missed its primary endpoint in December 2025 and Biohaven has announced it is
discontinuing the psychiatry program. Study status should be verified before enrollment.

sections.title: MECHANISM OF ACTION
sections.body:
Kv7.2/7.3 potassium-channel opener. Reduces neuronal hyperexcitability. Distinct from XEN1101
(azetukalner) but same target class.

sections.title: STUDY DESIGN DEEP DIVE
sections.body:
Open-label extension, single-arm, safety-focused. Primary: incidence of TEAEs over 52 weeks. Rollover
from Phase 2 parent studies.

sections.title: RECRUIT IF YOUR PATIENT…
sections.body:
Adults who completed a BHV-7000 MDD parent trial. The whole point: generate the long-term safety
database NDAs require.

sections.title: STOP / EXCLUDE IF…
sections.body:
7 New-onset suicidality
7 Seizure disorder or epilepsy
7 QTc >470 ms
7 Pregnancy/nursing
7 Active substance use

sections.title: IDEAL PATIENT PROFILE
sections.body: Prior completer from BHV-7000 Phase 2 MDD parent study.

sections.title: HOW IPMG FINDS THESE PATIENTS (ECW + ICD-10)
sections.body: eCW: cross-reference prior Biohaven study participants at your site. Limited external recruitment.

sections.title: COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE
sections.body:
Azetukalner (XEN1101, Neurocrine) is the other major Kv7 psychiatry asset. Both face uncertainty after
BHV-7000 Phase 2 MDD failure.

sections.title: RISK / BENEFIT LOGIC
sections.body:
Benefit: novel ion-channel mechanism, if signal emerges. Risk: program discontinuation likely means no
path to label. Discuss status with sponsor before dosing new patients.

sections.title: WHY THIS MATTERS FOR A LARGE PSYCHIATRIC GROUP
sections.body:
LTS studies are low-effort, high-retention revenue. Even a discontinued program pays site overhead while
patients complete.

sections.title: PROVIDER TALK TRACK (6TH–7TH GRADE)
sections.body:
“This is a safety study for patients already in our depression research. We're making sure the medicine
is safe when taken for a full year.”

sections.title: THE HOOK
sections.body:
STILL giving 14-year-olds with first-episode psychosis the same D2 blockers we
used in 1975?

sections.title: THE UNMET NEED
sections.body:
Pediatric schizophrenia is devastating and under-served. Metabolic side effects from D2 antipsychotics drive
50%+ non-adherence in teens.
H A non-D2, M1/M4-selective muscarinic could be the first truly new antipsychotic class ever
labeled in adolescents.

sections.title: AT A GLANCE
sections.body:
13-17 6 wks Flexible ~400
AGE DB DOSE TARGET N

sections.title: WHY THIS STUDY EXISTS
sections.body:
BMS acquired Karuna for $14B largely for KarXT (Cobenfy). Pediatric label expansion is the highest-value
follow-on.

sections.title: MECHANISM OF ACTION
sections.body:
Xanomeline (M1/M4 agonist) + trospium (peripheral muscarinic antagonist that blocks GI side effects without
crossing BBB).

sections.title: STUDY DESIGN DEEP DIVE
sections.body:
Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled flex-dose 6 weeks. Primary: PANSS total change. Open-label
extension follows.

sections.title: RECRUIT IF YOUR PATIENT…
sections.body:
13-17 y/o, DSM-5 schizophrenia, PANSS >=80, stable housing, guardian-consented. The whole point:
prevent the metabolic catastrophe D2 antipsychotics create in adolescents.

sections.title: STOP / EXCLUDE IF…
sections.body:
7 Known narrow-angle glaucoma
7 Urinary retention/severe constipation
7 Substance-induced psychosis
7 Pregnancy/nursing
7 Ongoing homicidality

sections.title: IDEAL PATIENT PROFILE
sections.body: 16 y/o first-episode, started risperidone 8 weeks ago, gained 14 lb, refusing further dose increases.

sections.title: HOW IPMG FINDS THESE PATIENTS (ECW + ICD-10)
sections.body: eCW: ICD-10 F20.x + age 13-17 + antipsychotic in last 90 days. Flag BMI increase >=5% in 6 months.

sections.title: COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE
sections.body:
Risperidone, aripiprazole, paliperidone, lurasidone all have pediatric SCZ labels. KarXT differentiates on
non-D2 mechanism + no weight/metabolic signal.

sections.title: RISK / BENEFIT LOGIC
sections.body:
Benefit: first non-D2 antipsychotic for teens, no weight gain/EPS. Risk: cholinergic side effects (nausea, dry
mouth), anticholinergic from trospium (constipation).

sections.title: WHY THIS MATTERS FOR A LARGE PSYCHIATRIC GROUP
sections.body: IPMG's pediatric psychiatry volume is rare. Sites that can enroll pediatric psychosis are a national asset.

sections.title: PROVIDER TALK TRACK (6TH–7TH GRADE)
sections.body:
“This is a brand-new kind of medicine for teenagers with schizophrenia. It's not like the older pills that
cause weight gain. It works on a different brain chemical.”

title: KarXT BALSAM-4 in Bipolar I Mania

sections.title: THE HOOK
sections.body:
STILL watching bipolar patients on Lithium or Depakote break through into mania
with no adjunct option that won't wreck them metabolically?

sections.title: THE UNMET NEED
sections.body:
Acute bipolar mania breakthrough on a mood stabilizer is a daily psychiatric problem. Every adjunctive
atypical brings a metabolic penalty that kills adherence post-discharge.
H If KarXT controls breakthrough mania as an ADJUNCT to lithium, valproate, or lamotrigine
without weight gain, it becomes the default post-hospital bridge.

sections.title: AT A GLANCE
sections.body:
18-65 3 wks Adjunct only ~400
AGE DB DESIGN TARGET N

sections.title: WHY THIS STUDY EXISTS
sections.body:
BMS's adult bipolar-mania expansion for Cobenfy as an ADJUNCT to standard mood stabilizers.
Complements the SCZ label and the adolescent program.

sections.title: MECHANISM OF ACTION
sections.body: Same as Study 7 — M1/M4 agonist + peripheral muscarinic antagonist.

sections.title: STUDY DESIGN DEEP DIVE
sections.body:
Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled ADJUNCTIVE 3-week acute mania study. Patients must
currently be taking — and remain on throughout the trial — lithium, valproate (divalproex), or lamotrigine as
their background mood stabilizer. KarXT or placebo is added on top. Primary: YMRS change at Day 21.

sections.title: RECRUIT IF YOUR PATIENT…
sections.body:
Adults 18-65 with DSM-5 BP-I, current manic or mixed episode, YMRS >=20, AND currently taking lithium,
valproate, or lamotrigine (must be continued throughout the study). The whole point: adjunctive rescue
without piling on metabolic burden.

sections.title: STOP / EXCLUDE IF…
sections.body:
7 Patient NOT currently on lithium, valproate, or lamotrigine (they must stay on background stabilizer)
7 Mixed features with severe depression
7 Rapid cycling
7 Substance-induced mania
7 Pregnancy
7 History of narrow-angle glaucoma

sections.title: IDEAL PATIENT PROFILE
sections.body:
29 y/o BP-I on lithium 900 mg daily for the past year, now in a manic episode (YMRS 28), needs adjunctive
treatment but doesn't want to add olanzapine due to weight gain history.

sections.title: HOW IPMG FINDS THESE PATIENTS (ECW + ICD-10)
sections.body:
eCW: F31.1x/F31.2 + ACTIVE lithium, valproate/divalproex, OR lamotrigine prescription + recent
manic/mixed features documented. These are the ONLY eligible patients.

sections.title: COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE
sections.body:
Olanzapine, risperidone, quetiapine, aripiprazole, asenapine, Vraylar all labeled as adjuncts for mania.
KarXT's weight-neutrality is the unique selling point within the adjunct space.

sections.title: RISK / BENEFIT LOGIC
sections.body:
Benefit: mania control as an adjunct to standard mood stabilizer, without atypical metabolic burden. Risk:
cholinergic GI side effects acute; efficacy vs olanzapine gold standard unknown.

sections.title: WHY THIS MATTERS FOR A LARGE PSYCHIATRIC GROUP
sections.body:
Mania studies require inpatient/24-hour-accessible sites AND patients already established on a mood
stabilizer. IPMG's footprint supports both.

sections.title: PROVIDER TALK TRACK (6TH–7TH GRADE)
sections.body:
“This is a new add-on medicine for bipolar mania. You'd keep taking your lithium (or Depakote or
Lamictal), and we'd add this or a placebo on top. It works differently from Zyprexa and doesn't cause
the big weight gain.”

sections.body: See Study 7. Additional hypothesis: M1 agonism may also slow cognitive decline via cholinergic restoration.

title: KarXT + KarX-EC ADEPT-5 in Alzheimer's Psychosis

sections.title: THE HOOK
sections.body:
STILL wishing you could spread dementia-psychosis dosing over the whole day
without 5 pills?

sections.title: THE UNMET NEED
sections.body:
KarXT IR dosing = BID; GI tolerability in elderly is the rate-limiter. An extended-release (KarX-EC) could
unlock this population.
H Once-daily KarX-EC could be the ultimate geriatric-friendly formulation.

sections.title: AT A GLANCE
sections.body:
55-90 12 wks QD EC ~500
AGE DB DOSING TARGET N

sections.title: WHY THIS STUDY EXISTS
sections.body:
Formulation bridge study. Pairs IR (KarXT) vs EC (KarX-EC) to demonstrate equivalence and support QD
label in AD.

sections.title: MECHANISM OF ACTION
sections.body: Same xanomeline + trospium; extended-release formulation reduces peak/trough variability.

sections.title: STUDY DESIGN DEEP DIVE
sections.body:
Randomized, double-blind, placebo + active comparator (IR vs EC). Primary: NPI-C hallucinations +
delusions.

sections.title: THE UNMET NEED
sections.body:
Adjunct MDD is dominated by atypical antipsychotics that carry metabolic and EPS risk. A cleaner AMPA
modulator could redefine augmentation.
H AMPA positive allosteric modulation is the long-sought glutamate angle — osavampator's
Phase 2 SAVITRI HIT validates the class.

sections.title: AT A GLANCE
sections.body:
18-65 6 wks QD dosing ~500
AGE DB TARGET N

sections.title: WHY THIS STUDY EXISTS
sections.body:
Phase 2 SAVITRI showed statistically significant MADRS separation vs placebo in adjunct MDD. Phase 3
SAVITRI-3 is registrational.

sections.title: MECHANISM OF ACTION
sections.body:
AMPA receptor positive allosteric modulator (PAM). Enhances fast excitatory glutamate signaling without
NMDA/ketamine dissociative profile.

sections.title: STUDY DESIGN DEEP DIVE
sections.body: Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled adjunct to ongoing antidepressant. Primary: MADRS Week 6.

sections.title: RECRUIT IF YOUR PATIENT…
sections.body:
MDD adults on stable antidepressant >=6 weeks, MADRS >=24, partial response. The whole point:
glutamate augmentation without ketamine's baggage.

sections.title: STOP / EXCLUDE IF…

7 TRD (>=2 failed trials this episode typically)
7 Bipolar
7 Substance use
7 Seizure history
7 Pregnancy

sections.body:
Aripiprazole, brexpiprazole, olanzapine-fluoxetine, Caplyta, Rexulti ADMDD space. Osavampator's non-D2
mechanism + Phase 2 HIT = strong differentiator.

sections.title: RISK / BENEFIT LOGIC
sections.body:
Benefit: novel glutamate mechanism, clean Phase 2 signal. Risk: unknown long-term cognitive effects;
AMPA overstimulation carries seizure-risk theoretical concern.

sections.title: WHY THIS MATTERS FOR A LARGE PSYCHIATRIC GROUP

Adjunct MDD is every psychiatrist's daily problem. Carrying a differentiated asset here builds KOL
reputation.

title: SPT-300 (GlyphAllo) MDD with Anxious Distress — BUOY-1

sections.title: THE HOOK
sections.body:
STILL watching 'depression with anxious distress' patients fail both SSRI and
benzo paths?

sections.title: THE UNMET NEED
sections.body:
DSM-5 MDD 'with anxious distress' specifier predicts SSRI non-response. No mechanism-matched drug
exists.
H An oral allopregnanolone prodrug could deliver Zulresso-like GABAA modulation
outpatient, targeting the anxious subset.

sections.title: AT A GLANCE
sections.body:
18-65 8 wks Oral QD ~250
AGE DB TARGET N

sections.title: WHY THIS STUDY EXISTS
sections.body:
Seaport (spin-out from PureTech) is advancing SPT-300 — an oral allopregnanolone prodrug — for MDD
with anxious features. BUOY-1 is the lead efficacy trial.

sections.title: MECHANISM OF ACTION
sections.body:
Glycolipid-conjugated allopregnanolone prodrug (GlyphAllo). Slow-release oral delivery of the same active
as brexanolone/zuranolone.

sections.title: STUDY DESIGN DEEP DIVE
sections.body: Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled 8 weeks. Primary: HAM-D17 change; key secondary: HAM-A.

sections.title: RECRUIT IF YOUR PATIENT…
sections.body:
Adults with MDD + DSM-5 anxious distress specifier (>=2 of 5 anxious features). The whole point: target the
anxious-depressed subset that SSRIs fail.

sections.title: STOP / EXCLUDE IF…
sections.body:
7 Bipolar
7 Active SUD
7 Recent benzodiazepine/hypnotic use
7 Pregnancy
7 Severe hepatic dysfunction

sections.title: IDEAL PATIENT PROFILE
sections.body: 38 y/o MDD with panic-like somatic anxiety, tried escitalopram + buspirone, still HAM-D 22.

sections.title: HOW IPMG FINDS THESE PATIENTS (ECW + ICD-10)
sections.body: eCW: F32.x/F33.x + F41.1 (GAD)/F41.9 comorbid. Flag PHQ + GAD-7 both elevated.

sections.title: COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE
sections.body:
Zuranolone (Zurzuvae) is approved for PPD only. SSRI + benzo is the default messy combo. SPT-300 aims
to replace both.

sections.title: RISK / BENEFIT LOGIC
sections.body:
Benefit: GABAA mechanism matched to anxious-depressed subtype, no scheduled status expected. Risk:
somnolence/sedation class AEs.

sections.title: WHY THIS MATTERS FOR A LARGE PSYCHIATRIC GROUP
sections.body: Anxious depression is the MOST common MDD subtype. Target-able, screenable, and under-treated.

“This pill is based on a new way to treat depression — especially the kind where anxiety is also a big
problem. It's an oral version of a medicine used after childbirth.”

sections.title: THE HOOK
sections.body:
STILL balancing lithium toxicity and lamotrigine rashes as 'mood stabilizer
options' in 2026?

sections.title: THE UNMET NEED
sections.body:
Mood stabilizer innovation has stalled since Depakote. Novel targets are desperately needed.
H TARP-gamma8-dependent AMPA modulation offers region-selective glutamate tuning — a
fundamentally new bipolar mechanism.

sections.title: AT A GLANCE
sections.body:
18-65 8 wks QD ~200
AGE DB DOSING TARGET N

sections.title: WHY THIS STUDY EXISTS
sections.body: Rapport's lead asset, entering bipolar mood episodes. Phase 2a proof-of-concept in BP-I.

sections.title: MECHANISM OF ACTION
sections.body:
Selective TARP-gamma8-dependent AMPA receptor negative allosteric modulator. Targets
hippocampal/cortical AMPA without global glutamate suppression.

sections.title: STUDY DESIGN DEEP DIVE
sections.body:
Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled 8 weeks. Primary: YMRS (for mania-inclusive cohort) or
MADRS (for depressed cohort).

sections.title: RECRUIT IF YOUR PATIENT…
sections.body:
BP-I adults in current episode. The whole point: rescue patients whose mood stabilizer regimen isn't working
without adding atypical metabolic burden.

sections.title: STOP / EXCLUDE IF…
sections.body:
7 Rapid cycling
7 Psychotic features
7 Substance use
7 Seizure history (AMPA NAM could theoretically alter threshold — but mechanistically seizure-neutral)
7 Pregnancy

sections.body: eCW: F31.1x/F31.2/F31.3x/F31.4 + prior mood stabilizer failure.

sections.title: RISK / BENEFIT LOGIC
sections.body:
Benefit: truly new mechanism; region-selective = fewer side effects predicted. Risk: Phase 2, unproven
efficacy; glutamatergic class carries seizure-threshold questions.

title: Lumateperone Pediatric SCZ / Bipolar / Autism Safety

sections.title: THE HOOK
sections.body: STILL prescribing risperidone to 8-year-olds because nothing cleaner is labeled?

sections.title: THE UNMET NEED
sections.body:
Pediatric psychiatric labels are thin, and weight/metabolic safety in kids on D2 blockers is dire.
H Lumateperone's clean adult profile (virtually no weight gain) makes it the most anticipated
pediatric antipsychotic launch since risperidone.

sections.title: AT A GLANCE
sections.body:
6-17 52 wks Flex dose ~300
AGE SAFETY TARGET N

sections.title: WHY THIS STUDY EXISTS
sections.body:
J&J; closed its $14.6B Intra-Cellular acquisition April 2025. Caplyta adjunctive MDD approved Nov 6, 2025.
Pediatric expansion across SCZ/BP/autism is J&J;'s next move.

sections.title: MECHANISM OF ACTION
sections.body:
Serotonin/dopamine/glutamate modulator. Unique presynaptic D2 agonism + postsynaptic D2 antagonism +
5-HT2A antagonism + glutamate modulation.

sections.title: STUDY DESIGN DEEP DIVE
sections.body:
Long-term open-label safety in pediatric populations spanning schizophrenia, bipolar, and autism. Primary:
safety/tolerability; secondary: efficacy signals.

sections.title: RECRUIT IF YOUR PATIENT…
sections.body:
6-17 y/o with DSM-5 SCZ, BP-I (dep or mania), or autism with severe behavioral disturbance. The whole
point: give kids the cleanest antipsychotic safety data available.

sections.title: STOP / EXCLUDE IF…
sections.body:
7 Severe comorbid medical illness
7 Active substance use (older adolescents)
7 Pregnancy/nursing
7 Recent major treatment change

sections.title: IDEAL PATIENT PROFILE

sections.title: COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE
sections.body:
Risperidone, aripiprazole, olanzapine, paliperidone all pediatric-labeled. Caplyta is positioned as the
cleanest metabolic profile.

sections.title: RISK / BENEFIT LOGIC
sections.body:
Benefit: clean adult safety profile, J&J; resources behind pediatric rollout. Risk: pediatric-specific AEs
(growth, puberty) still being defined.

sections.title: WHY THIS MATTERS FOR A LARGE PSYCHIATRIC GROUP

sections.title: THE UNMET NEED
sections.body:
Kappa opioid antagonism targets anhedonia and stress reactivity — two SSRI-resistant domains.

discontinuation (Mar 2025), icalcaprant is the last KOR bet.

sections.body:
AbbVie is picking up the KOR-antagonism baton after competitors failed. Better selectivity/dosing profile
claimed.

sections.title: MECHANISM OF ACTION
sections.body:
Selective kappa opioid receptor (KOR) antagonist. Blocks dynorphin-driven stress/anhedonia circuits
without mu/delta opioid activity.

sections.title: STUDY DESIGN DEEP DIVE
sections.body:
Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled monotherapy or adjunct (check protocol). Primary: MADRS
change; key secondary: SHAPS anhedonia.

sections.body:
MDD adults, MADRS >=26, anhedonia (SHAPS elevated). Patients with up to 2 failed antidepressant trials
this episode ARE eligible — only those with >=3 failures are excluded. The whole point: treat the 'nothing
feels good' depression that SSRIs miss.

7 Opioid use disorder
7 Chronic pain on opioids


7 >=3 failed antidepressant trials this episode (2 failures are OK)

title: Solriamfetol in Binge Eating Disorder — ENGAGE

sections.title: THE HOOK
sections.body:
STILL telling BED patients their only medication option is a Schedule II
amphetamine?

sections.title: THE UNMET NEED
sections.body:
BED is the most common eating disorder in the US — ~3 million adults. Lisdexamfetamine (Vyvanse) is the
ONLY FDA-approved drug, and its Schedule II status blocks access for countless patients with anxiety, SUD
history, or cardiovascular concerns.
H A Schedule IV, non-amphetamine DNRI+TAAR1 agonist that treats BED without
controlled-substance burden would democratize pharmacotherapy for a fundamentally
under-treated disorder.

sections.title: AT A GLANCE
sections.body:
18-55 12 wks 150/300 mg ~450
AGE DB DOSE TARGET N

sections.title: WHY THIS STUDY EXISTS
sections.body:
Axsome is leveraging Sunosi's existing safety/PK database to enter the $1B+ BED market. Schedule IV
status, once-daily dosing, and cardiovascular profile matured in narcolepsy/OSA trials position solriamfetol
as a first-line alternative to Vyvanse.

sections.title: MECHANISM OF ACTION
sections.body:
Dopamine-norepinephrine reuptake inhibitor (DNRI) + trace amine-associated receptor 1 (TAAR1) agonist.
Modulates mesolimbic reward circuits implicated in binge episodes without the Schedule II amphetamine
profile. Once-daily oral.

sections.title: STUDY DESIGN DEEP DIVE
sections.body:
Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, fixed-dose Phase 3. Three arms 1:1:1 — placebo,
solriamfetol 150 mg, solriamfetol 300 mg. 12 weeks double-blind + optional open-label extension. Primary:
change in binge eating days per week at Week 12 (DSM-5 criteria). Key secondary: CGI-S-BED, remission
rate, weight change, Yale-Brown Obsessive Compulsive Scale modified for BED (Y-BOCS-BE).

sections.title: RECRUIT IF YOUR PATIENT…
sections.body:
Adults 18-55 with DSM-5 BED, >=3 binge days per week x 14 days baseline, stable weight, medically
cleared cardiovascular status. The whole point: give BED patients a legitimate non-Schedule-II option and
prove solriamfetol's second indication.

sections.title: STOP / EXCLUDE IF…
sections.body:
7 Anorexia nervosa or bulimia nervosa past 5 years
7 Current purging behavior
7 Substance use disorder past 6 months
7 Uncontrolled hypertension (systolic >140, diastolic >90)
7 MAOI use past 14 days
7 Pregnancy/nursing
7 Bariatric surgery past 2 years
7 Concurrent stimulant or weight-loss medication

sections.title: IDEAL PATIENT PROFILE
sections.body:
32 y/o female with 2 years of BED, 4-5 binge days/week, anxious about Vyvanse due to family history of
stimulant misuse; medically healthy.

sections.title: HOW IPMG FINDS THESE PATIENTS (ECW + ICD-10)
sections.body:
eCW: ICD-10 F50.81 (BED) OR F50.9 eating disorder NOS + body-weight complaints or 'binge'
documentation. Cross-reference therapist referrals; flag patients declining Vyvanse for Schedule-II reasons.

sections.title: COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE
sections.body:
Lisdexamfetamine (Vyvanse, Takeda) is the only FDA-approved BED drug — Schedule II. Off-label: SSRIs,
topiramate, naltrexone-bupropion. Solriamfetol's Schedule IV status + proven cardiovascular database is the
clear strategic differentiator.

sections.title: RISK / BENEFIT LOGIC
sections.body:
Benefit: first non-amphetamine approved BED option; leverages mature safety data from Sunosi
narcolepsy/OSA indication; likely once-daily, oral, without DEA Schedule II constraints. Risk: BP/HR
elevation (class effect), insomnia if dosed late, headache, nausea — manageable with dose titration.

sections.title: WHY THIS MATTERS FOR A LARGE PSYCHIATRIC GROUP
sections.body:
BED is underdiagnosed — estimated only 3% of affected adults receive a formal diagnosis. A large
psychiatric group with integrated nutrition and therapy referrals can systematically screen with Y-BOCS-BE
and Binge Eating Scale at intake, creating a dedicated BED recruitment pipeline that competes with
academic eating-disorder centers.

sections.title: PROVIDER TALK TRACK (6TH–7TH GRADE)
sections.body:
“Binge eating disorder has only one approved medicine, and it's a controlled substance some people
can't take. We're studying a different medicine — already approved for sleepiness — that works without
the controlled-substance rules. It's once a day.”

sections.title: THE HOOK
sections.body:
STILL watching SSRI-only PTSD care leave combat vets and assault survivors
with lifelong flashbacks?

sections.title: THE UNMET NEED
sections.body:
PTSD has only two FDA-approved drugs — sertraline and paroxetine — both from the 1990s with ~60%
non-response. After Lykos Therapeutics' MDMA-assisted therapy NDA was rejected August 2024, the field
lost its frontrunner. TSND-201 is the first post-Lykos neuroplastogen in registrational PTSD development.
H A rapid-acting, non-hallucinogenic neuroplastogen that drives MDMA-like empathogenic
and neuroplastic effects in a controlled 4-dose regimen could re-open the
psychedelic-adjacent PTSD paradigm the field thought was closed.

sections.title: AT A GLANCE
sections.body:
>=18 12 wks 4 weekly doses ~300
AGE TOTAL TARGET N

sections.title: WHY THIS STUDY EXISTS
sections.body:
Phase 2 IMPACT-1 demonstrated -9.64 CAPS-5 change vs placebo at Day 64 (p=0.01), NNT=3 for
response and NNT=5 for remission (JAMA Psychiatry, February 2026). FDA granted Breakthrough Therapy
designation July 2025. EMPOWER-1 is the pivotal Phase 3 confirmatory trial.

sections.title: MECHANISM OF ACTION
sections.body:
Methylone (3,4-methylenedioxy-N-methylcathinone) — a synthetic cathinone and 'neuroplastogen.'
Monoamine (5-HT, DA, NE) releaser with empathogenic effects similar to MDMA but shorter-acting and
non-hallucinogenic. Drives rapid synaptic plasticity and memory reconsolidation in trauma circuits.

sections.title: STUDY DESIGN DEEP DIVE
sections.body:
Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled Phase 3. ~300 patients, weekly dosing x 4 weeks + 8 weeks
follow-up = 12 weeks total. Primary: CAPS-5 change from baseline at Week 12. Key secondary: PCL-5,
CGI-I, remission rate, functional outcomes.

sections.title: RECRUIT IF YOUR PATIENT…
sections.body:
Adults >=18 with DSM-5 PTSD, CAPS-5 >=28 at screening, symptoms >=3 months, medically stable, willing
to undergo 4 dosing sessions with therapist support. The whole point: deliver the first new-mechanism PTSD
drug since 2001 and restore confidence in the psychedelic-adjacent field after Lykos.

sections.title: STOP / EXCLUDE IF…
sections.body:
7 Personal/1st-degree family history of psychosis, schizophrenia, or bipolar I
7 Active SUD (past 6 months, except nicotine)
7 Serious cardiovascular disease or uncontrolled hypertension
7 Active suicidal plan/intent (C-SSRS 4-5)
7 Pregnancy/nursing
7 Concurrent MAOI or serotonergic drug requiring washout
7 Recent psychedelic use

sections.title: IDEAL PATIENT PROFILE
sections.body:
34 y/o combat veteran with 8 years of treatment-resistant PTSD, failed sertraline + prazosin +
trauma-focused CBT, no family psychosis history, medically healthy, open to dosing-day psychological
support.

sections.title: HOW IPMG FINDS THESE PATIENTS (ECW + ICD-10)
sections.body:
eCW: ICD-10 F43.10 (PTSD) + PCL-5 >=33 most recent + on/off SSRI + no F20.x/F31.x/F25.x. Flag VA
referrals and cases where prazosin or propranolol were prescribed for nightmares.

sections.title: COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE
sections.body:
Sertraline (Zoloft) and paroxetine (Paxil) — only approved drugs, both 1990s. MDMA-assisted therapy
(Lykos) NDA rejected August 2024. BNC210 (Bionomics), brexpiprazole-sertraline combo, and CYB004
(Cybin, DMT) are other late-stage PTSD candidates. TSND-201's non-hallucinogenic profile + Breakthrough
designation + published Phase 2 = category leader.

sections.title: RISK / BENEFIT LOGIC
sections.body:
Benefit: first novel-mechanism PTSD drug in 25 years, category-defining Phase 2 data, NNT=3 response,
FDA Breakthrough status, clinician-feasible dosing (no hallucinations). Risk: class-effect BP/HR elevation,
transient anxiety at dosing, potential for abuse liability assessment by DEA, operational staffing for 4
monitored dosing days.

sections.title: WHY THIS MATTERS FOR A LARGE PSYCHIATRIC GROUP
sections.body:
PTSD is endemic in IPMG's catchment — large military/veteran population in Southern California, plus
routine trauma exposure in first responders and assault survivors. A site offering the FIRST new-mechanism
PTSD trial since the SSRI era will become the regional referral center and position IPMG as the
category-defining PTSD research group.

sections.title: PROVIDER TALK TRACK (6TH–7TH GRADE)
sections.body:
“PTSD has had the same two medicines for 25 years. This is a brand-new kind — not an SSRI, not a
hallucinogen, but a medicine that helps the brain rewire the trauma memory. Four dosing days with a
therapist. Phase 3 — the last step before FDA approval.”

sections.title: 3-STEP RECRUIT MOVE
sections.body:
n TARGET. Run the eCW ICD-10 query above; pull the last 3 months of matching visits.
n FLAG. Mark eligible charts with a sticky note or task for the PMHNP team. Tag with the comment code.
% CALL. IPMG Research at (909) 955-5865 and reference comment EMPOWER1.
